codeReview.summary: The feature implements the intended net/http namespace slice, but it has two blocking correctness gaps: codegen treats any `http::...` path as stdlib even without an import, and imported stdlib types are incorrectly accepted in expression position and lower to invalid Go.

issues.description: `is_imported_package` is hardcoded to `name = "http"`, so codegen reinterprets any local `http::...` path as the stdlib package instead of consulting resolver/typecheck import state. A local declaration such as `enum http { Ok }` compiles to Go that imports `net/http` and emits `http.Ok`, which then fails downstream (`/tmp/review_http_local_enum.go`: `http already declared through import of package http`, `undefined: http.Ok`). This breaks the namespace-collision contract for non-imported local symbols.

`check_stdlib_path` returns `TImported` for `StdlibType`, so imported types are accepted as value expressions instead of being rejected outside type position. `use net::http; let x = http::Request;` currently compiles and codegen lowers it to `x := http.Request`, which Go rejects as `http.Request (type) is not an expression` (`/tmp/review_http_type_value.go`). This means the feature still accepts programs that cannot lower to valid Go.

sharedStateObservations.observation: `compiler-worker` should explicitly require a regression where a local declaration shares the stdlib alias without importing it, so naming-bridge work proves it is driven by resolved import state rather than raw identifier text.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The handoff/test list only mentions positive net/http coverage plus wrong-case, unqualified, and unknown-member fixtures. There is no fixture for a local `http` type/enum without `use net::http`, and the current implementation miscompiles `enum http { Ok } ... http::Ok` because `lib/codegen.ml:277` hardcodes the alias.

summary: Reviewed handoff, transcript skeleton, skill, and commit `d5ed6d5`. Result: fail. The feature mostly wires the intended stdlib namespace slice, but two blocking bugs remain: local `http::...` paths are mis-lowered as net/http even without an import, and imported stdlib types are incorrectly allowed in expression position, producing invalid Go.
